projectDescription: A Fourier-Featured Physics-Informed Neural Network framework for fast, accurate simulation of acoustic particle agglomeration, offering orders-of-magnitude speedup over FEM and DEM baselines.
keyFeatures: 5-branch factorized PINN replacing costly FEM/DEM force evaluations
Random Fourier Feature encoding tuned to acoustic wavenumber and frequency
Symplectic-Euler time integration for stable long-horizon particle tracking
CPU and CUDA GPU support with automatic device detection
Multiple particle initialization modes: linear, random, uniform, sample, normal
Pre-trained model weights for 8, 10, 12, and 16 kHz standing-wave setups
COMSOL FEM and DEM validation with <0.01% concentration error
Orthokinetic collision-kernel extraction for agglomeration rate estimation
Orders-of-magnitude runtime reduction: ~13× vs. DEM, ~27× vs. COMSOL FEM
Deterministic simulations via fixed random seeds
publicApiSurface: demo_input.json (config schema: n_steps, dt_s, target_time_s, pinn_model_base_path, x_window_mm, kde_sigma_mm, kde_n_grid)
